h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: if the clerk records it later beside the complaint file, maybe it’s doing pressure instead of promise?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was initially seeking a single straightforward detail to prove the sentence's function as pressure.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
[turns the attachment slip first toward the mail tray, then toward the claims desk]

In the tray, “attached” only keeps papers moving together. At the claims desk, the same slip changes status if it says “for response,” names an office, or requires a reply.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor uses physical object work and spatial representation to clarify the ambiguous status of the attachment mark, adapting to the learner's specific confusion about circulation versus use.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: That distinction is the hinge. Put the actual mark on trial: stamp, attachment, routing line, addressee, or “cited in response.”
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Following the learner's realization in L2, the tutor shifts the task from finding any proof to putting the specific type of mark on trial to distinguish custody from use.
